reasoning: The resolution criteria require two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth between Q1 2024 and Q4 2025. Q1 2024 to Q4 2024 were all firmly positive (e.g., Q4 2024 third estimate was +2.4%). Q1 2025 was negative (-0.5%), but Q2 2025 has just been revised UP to +3.3% in its second estimate (released August 28, 2025). This means Q1 and Q2 are not consecutive negative quarters, and Q2 breaks any chain. Thus, the only remaining possibilities for a recession counting under this criteria are Q3 2025 AND Q4 2025 both being negative. However, current Atlanta Fed GDPNow tracking for Q3 2025 is solid (+2.3% as of August 19). For the resolution to trigger, Q3 2025 would need to unexpectedly turn deeply negative, Q4 2025 would also need to be negative, and this would have to withstand the BEA's final estimates. Furthermore, an alternative path—a massive downward revision of Q2 2025 from +3.3% into negative territory—is virtually unprecedented outside of external shocks that are not currently occurring. The market price at 12% on August 21 likely reflected some residual uncertainty before the late August Q2 update, but based on the +3.3% figure and positive Q3 tracking, the probability of two consecutive negative quarters by the end of 2025 is extremely low. I am setting it at the minimum allowable reasonable bound, 0.02.
